ralph_core_truths.what_is_it: Ralph Workflow is the operating system for autonomous coding: a free and open-source composable loop framework and AI orchestrator.
ralph_core_truths.who_is_it_for: Developers and technical teams doing ambitious software work that benefits from a structured workflow instead of a chat session.
ralph_core_truths.why_different: It keeps a simple Ralph-loop core, then composes that core into planning, development, verification, and broader workflow loops with strong defaults.
ralph_core_truths.why_now: You can use the default workflow as-is today, or build your own workflow on top without giving up control of your tools or process.

findings.name: awesome-cli-coding-agents (bradAGI)
findings.what: Canonical 2026 curated directory of 80+ CLI coding agents, harnesses, and orchestrators. Last updated 2026-06-01. 1.5k+ stars.
findings.note: Other 'ralph' entries are unrelated projects (ralph-orchestrator 2.9k, ralph-tui 2.3k, wreckit 128).
findings.action: PR prepared — blocked on gh auth login. Asset: drafts/awesome_list_pr_ready_2026-06-04.md

findings.name: amux.io — Best Multi-Agent Coding Orchestrators in 2026
findings.what: Comprehensive comparison of 9 multi-agent orchestrators: Claude Squad, Conductor, Kilo Code, Agent Teams, Codex, Devin, Goose, dmux, amux. Evaluates parallel capacity, git isolation, self-healing, dashboard, task board, mobile, unattended operation, cost.
findings.relevant_gap: Ralph Workflow would score well on: unattended operation (full support), git isolation (worktrees), self-healing (checkpoint/resume), cost (free + MIT). Would be the only vendor-neutral, spec-driven entry that composes existing agents on local hardware.
findings.action: Contact amux for inclusion consideration. Post-hold (after June 5).

findings.name: augmentcode.com — AI Coding Orchestrators Comparison 2026
findings.what: Reviews 9 orchestrators: Composio AO, Emdash, Baton, Conductor family, Bernstein, Claude Squad, Crystal/Nimbalyst, Vibe Kanban, Agent Kanban.
findings.relevant_gap: Bernstein (540⭐) is the closest peer already listed — deterministic orchestrator, spawns parallel agents, verifies with tests, auto-commits. Ralph Workflow has stronger multi-phase loop + checkpoint/resume + vendor-neutral architecture.
findings.action: Contact augmentcode for inclusion. Post-hold (after June 5).

category_positioning.orchestrators_autonomous_loops: Ralph Workflow sits at 12⭐ in a section where peers range 540⭐ (Bernstein) to 3⭐ (sage). The section is growing fast — kodo (106⭐) was recently added. Ralph's differentiated positioning: vendor-neutral multi-phase unattended loop vs single-model orchestrators (Symphony=Codex-only, kodo=Claude-Code-Max-only).

reuse_contract.purpose: Shared market and competitor findings artifact for site updates, positioning checks, and marketing tactics.
